summary: The frontend container is a component‑based Angular application built with TypeScript, RxJS, and Angular Material, organized into presentation, service, integration, core/shared, and routing layers. It implements the Deed Registry and Workflow Management bounded context, exposing capabilities such as user authentication, deed CRUD, workflow approval, digital signature validation, document archiving, notification handling, reporting, and extensive REST API integrations. While the architecture follows Angular best‑practice layering, moderate coupling, cohesion, and separation of concerns, technical debt signals oversized services, an excess of adapters and pipes, and insufficient module boundaries, resulting in a quality grade of C.

capabilities: Create, edit, view, and delete deeds
Interact with multiple RESTful backend services

debt_indicators: High count of pipes (67) indicating possible over‑use
recommendations: Refactor oversized services into smaller, domain‑focused services
Consolidate or group related adapters to reduce duplication
Review and prune pipes; combine where possible or replace with component logic
Introduce clearer module boundaries (feature modules) to enforce separation of concerns
Apply interface segregation principles to services and adapters
Implement architectural guidelines to limit component/service proliferation

overall_assessment: The frontend delivers the required business capabilities and follows a sensible Angular architecture, but its moderate coupling and cohesion together with several technical‑debt hotspots (large services, adapter explosion, pipe overuse) limit maintainability and scalability, placing it at a C grade.
top_recommendations: Refactor large services into focused, domain‑specific services
Consolidate and rationalize adapters to reduce duplication and surface area
Introduce well‑defined feature modules to improve separation of concerns and enforce interface segregation
